item.title: Share of Young Canadians Living with Parents Doubled, Study Says
item.description: Millennials were twice as likely to live with their parents in 2021 than baby boomers were at the same age, a trend that was particularly acute in expensive cities but not fully attributed to affordability challenges.

item.title: Israeli Central Bank Slams Move to Label Top Banks an Oligopoly
item.description: Israel’s central bank accused the competition watchdog of making an “extreme and disproportionate” decision following a move to categorize the country’s top lenders as an oligopoly.

item.title: Argentina Fights to Save Huge Soy Exports After Dutch Rejections
item.description: Argentina is scrambling to keep its soy cargoes flowing to Europe after an unapproved genetically modified strain was detected in shipments, raising the risk of widespread rejections of the country’s most valuable export.

item.title: Where to Invest Now That Moon Race 2.0 Is Here
item.description: As Morgan Stanley analysts put it, “space is back in a big way,” with renewed momentum spanning everything from raw materials and mining to fuel and propulsion systems.

item.title: World Cup Fans Get Site to Help Manage Northeast Transit Chaos
item.description: World Cup fans now have an online resource to help them navigate passenger rail options for getting to matches in the Northeast.

item.title: Renewable Firm Solor BioEnergi Weighs €4 Billion Sale
item.description: The owners of Solor Bioenergi are weighing a sale of the renewable energy supplier, which could be valued at around €4 billion ($4.7 billion), people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: Gasoline Surge Fuels a K-Shape Divide in US, Research Shows
item.description: The war-driven surge in gasoline prices has deepened a K-shape divide seen across the US economy, weighing disproportionately on lower-income households.

item.title: OPEC Output Falls to New 36-Year Low on Iran War, Survey Shows
item.description: OPEC’s crude production fell to a new 36-year low last month as the Iran war continued to choke off exports from the Persian Gulf and forced further shut-ins, according to a Bloomberg survey.

item.title: Google Makes EU Offer to Avert Fines in News Search Case
item.description: Google has proposed changes to how it displays news results across its search engine in a bid to avoid adding to its €9.5 billion ($11.2 billion) running total of European Union competition fines.

item.title: How Trump Has Made the Doctor Shortage Worse
item.description: The administration is backing off one part of its sweeping immigration crackdown on overseas physicians, but applicants worry it may be too late to meet the critical residency and fellowship start date on July 1.

item.title: CME Group to Launch US Dollar Overnight Repo Benchmark
item.description: CME Group Inc. on Wednesday launched a new benchmark rate tied to overnight funding costs in the US, adding to its suite of global risk-free rates.

item.title: US Exports of Oil Products Reach Weekly Record, EIA Says
item.description: US exports of oil products rose to a record high last week at 8.2 million barrels a day.

item.title: Underdog Hires Former Head of Crypto.com Prediction Exchange
item.description: Sports-centric predictions market platform Underdog has hired Nick Lundgren, the previous head of Crypto.com’s event contracts exchange, to serve as chief legal officer.

item.title: Fed’s Musalem Says Risks Are Shifting More Toward Inflation
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis President Alberto Musalem said there is much uncertainty over the future path for the economy and monetary policy, but he sees risks rising more for inflation than for employment.

item.title: SpaceX Financials Flagged as Concern to SEC by Advocacy Group
item.description: SOC Investment Group, an adviser to union-affiliated pension funds, is urging US regulators to probe SpaceX’s financials ahead of a Wall Street debut that could value the company at more than $2 trillion, citing concerns over its relationships with Elon Musk’s other ventures.

item.title: UK Elections Threaten to End the Pound’s Resilient Run
item.description: Investor worries over higher energy prices and elections that look to be disastrous for the government pushed the UK’s long-term borrowing costs to the highest level since 1998 this week. The pound wasn’t fazed.

item.title: France Says Maritime Group Ready to Escort Ships in Hormuz
item.description: A maritime coalition led by France and the UK is ready to escort tankers through the Strait of Hormuz if Iran agrees to a US proposal to end the war, according to a French official.

item.title: Invesco Says Big Tech Needs ‘Every Dollar’ It Can Get in AI Debt
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s debt issuance in Europe and Canada underscores Big Tech’s relentless drive to find funds anywhere to pay for the industry’s stampede into artificial intelligence, according to Matt Brill at Invesco.

item.title: Ecuador Is Selling More Debt in Global Markets Amid Oil Windfall
item.description: Ecuador is offering more of the dollar bonds it first sold in January, taking advantage of higher oil prices and sustained investor appetite for emerging-market debt.

item.title: Iran War Boosts Russia’s April Oil Tax Revenue to Six-Month High
item.description: Russia’s oil tax revenues jumped to a six-month high last month as the Kremlin started reaping the benefits of a crude rally driven by the war in Iran. With oil still trading near $100 a barrel, the windfall is set to continue.

item.title: The World Is Scrambling to Remake Semiconductor Supply Chains
item.description: On this episode of Bloomberg Primer, we explain how companies and governments are rethinking where chips are made.

item.title: White House Prepares Order to Boost AI Security, Hassett Says
item.description: The White House is looking into an executive order that would create a vetting system for new artificial intelligence models like Anthropic PBC’s Mythos in a bid to protect business and government networks from AI-related cyber risks, a top economic adviser said Wednesday.

item.title: Sweden’s Finance Ministry Sees Recovery Slowed by Iran War
item.description: Sweden’s finance ministry expects the Nordic country’s economic growth to be hit harder by the Middle East war than seen previously, potentially denting the government’s reelection prospects four months out from a parliamentary vote.

item.title: Viral $100 Kids Landline Startup Tin Can Debuts Bulk Order Program
item.description: Tin Can Untechnologies Inc., the startup behind the $100 landline for kids, is launching a bulk order program to make it easier for schools, sports teams and neighborhoods to get access to the viral product.

item.title: Google DeepMind Takes Minority Stake in Maker of ‘Eve Online’
item.description: Artificial intelligence research is plunging further into video games.

item.title: Poland Holds Rates as Iran Energy Shock Stokes Inflation
item.description: Poland’s central bank kept interest rates unchanged for a second month on Wednesday as the energy-price impact of the Iran war revives long-benign domestic inflation.

item.title: Time to Fight the Fed? Stocks Rally Faces Dashed Rate-Cut Hopes
item.description: Is good news about the economy bad news for the stock market once again?

item.title: Greer Warns EU Tweaks Risk Watering Down US Trade Deal
item.description: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said changes the European Union is considering to a transatlantic trade deal could limit American exports to the bloc.

item.title: Shale Drillers Finally Heed Trump’s Call to Start Pumping More Oil
item.description: It’s taken a war in the Middle East and $100-a-barrel oil to convince companies.

item.title: Eli Lilly Kicks Off High-Grade Bond Sale in Push to Fund Deals
item.description: Eli Lilly & Co has kicked off the sale of investment-grade debt as part of an effort to fund its acquisition spree.
